meta_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Git with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Git with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use Git with AI to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. A beginner's guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real-world software fast.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you treat Git as a diary of AI-assisted engineering. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you can ship production-grade features rapidly. Consistent commits showcasing iterative AI-driven problem solving prove to recruiters that you can build, not just follow tutorials.
content.intro:
The blank screen stares back, a silent testament to the 'no portfolio' void. You have the ambition, but the gap between 'Hello World' and a production-ready application feels like a canyon. Every job posting demands experience you haven't been given the chance to earn, leaving you stuck in a loop of tutorials that never result in a tangible asset.

But the rules have changed. We are entering an era where leverage is everything. By mastering the intersection of Git and AI, you stop being a student who writes code and start being an engineer who builds systems. This isn't just about finishing a project; it's about documenting the evolution of a product so that when a hiring manager looks at your GitHub, they don't see beginner exercises—they see a professional workflow.

steps.title: Architecting the Vision
steps.description: Before writing code, define the core value proposition. Use AI to structure your directory and tech stack so the foundation is enterprise-ready from line one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-level architectural roadmap for a [insert project idea]. Define the directory structure, the primary tech stack, and the critical path to MVP, focusing on scalability and clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the initial project structure and README.md based on the architecture defined by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implementing the Core Feature
steps.description: Focus on the 'killer feature' that solves a real problem. Don't build generic apps; build tools that matter.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a modular implementation plan for the core feature: [insert feature]. Include test-driven development steps and error handling strategies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file ./src/core_feature.py --prompt 'Implement the core logic following the TDD approach defined in the roadmap.'

steps.title: Documenting the 'Why' in Git
steps.description: Your commit messages are your legacy. Use AI to summarize complex engineering decisions into clear, professional commit logs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my recent changes in [file]. Generate a concise, professional Git commit message that highlights the problem solved and the technical approach taken.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m "$(claude run --prompt 'Summarize these changes for a professional git commit message')"

steps.title: Refactoring for Performance
steps.description: AI is exceptional at spotting inefficiencies. Use it to audit your code for performance bottlenecks before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a code review on [file]. Identify potential performance bottlenecks, security vulnerabilities, and areas where readability can be improved.

steps.title: Building the Portfolio Narrative
steps.description: Your README is your landing page. Use AI to translate your technical commits into a compelling story for recruiters.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a professional README section titled 'Engineering Challenges' based on my git commit history. Emphasize the problem-solving process and the technical hurdles overcome.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit README.md --prompt 'Append the Engineering Challenges section generated by Antigravity.'

pro_tips: Treat your Git history as a narrative arc; ensure every commit moves the project closer to a solved problem.
Use AI to write your unit tests first; recruiters look for test coverage as a sign of professional maturity.
Don't hide the AI; explicitly document how you used AI to solve specific technical blockers to show you are an efficient, modern engineer.
Prioritize 'depth' over 'breadth'—one highly polished, documented project is worth ten incomplete 'to-do' list apps.
Automate your README updates using CI/CD pipelines so your documentation is never stale.
resources: Git Best Practices for Teams: https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, link the live demo in your README, and share your repository on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the technical decisions you made.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. You have the tools to build, the AI to scale your output, and the strategy to prove your value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Focus on the 'killer feature' that solves a real problem. Don't build generic apps; build tools that matter.
Your commit messages are your legacy. Use AI to summarize complex engineering decisions into clear, professional commit logs.
